title: Was every woman infertile at the time of 3I
display_title: Was every woman infertile at the time of 3I
wikitext:
[]: Was every woman infertile at the time of 3I?             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Wed Jan 03, 2007 8:24 pm I just remembered that i came up with this idea when i hear something along the lines of "3I will occur after the Chamber of Guf is empty/shut" ~OR~ "2I opened the Chamber of Guf, allowing for the Lilim" Ill develop the idea more when i have the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Jan 03, 2007 8:47 pm ep #17 wrote: OLD TEACHER: Thus, we, mankind, survived the hell on Earth called the Second Impact and rose again. The number of children has been decreasing yearly...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jan 03, 2007 9:34 pm A declining birthrate is mentioned in Evangelion, but if you know anything about the socio-economic problems of modern Japan, you will know that Japan's birth rate has been steadily declining since the 1980s. Currently it's one of the lowest in the world. Japan is such a competitive, over-crowded, costly place to live, that more and more people are choosing to either have only one child, or to have no children at all. Apparently in the world of Evangelion the second impact did nothing to aid Japan's birth rate.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Wed Jan 03, 2007 9:46 pm youd think thered be a boom after all the chaos...like with WWII... "You survived honey! Bang me!"

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jan 03, 2007 9:56 pm master0rolando wrote: youd think thered be a boom after all the chaos...like with WWII... "You survived honey! Bang me!"

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Wed Jan 03, 2007 10:08 pm Perhaps the Room of Guf isn't empty Perhaps they're scrapping the bottom of the barrel.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jan 03, 2007 11:05 pm MaggotMaster wrote: Perhaps they're scrapping the bottom of the barrel.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 04, 2007 12:37 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: No explicit reason is given for this. However it indicates children are still being born, albeit at slower rates.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 04, 2007 2:14 pm Reichu wrote: Anonymous_Evafan wrote: No explicit reason is given for this. However it indicates children are still being born, albeit at slower rates.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 04, 2007 2:33 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I think that would be reaching.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 04, 2007 3:44 pm Reichu wrote: Anonymous_Evafan wrote: No explicit reason is given for this. However it indicates children are still being born, albeit at slower rates.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 3:54 pm but still, i remember a line somewhere saying that the Chamber of Guf was empty, probably either in Death or EoE

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 04, 2007 3:56 pm InvaderRen wrote: but still, i remember a line somewhere saying that the Chamber of Guf was empty, probably either in Death or EoE

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 04, 2007 3:58 pm It was the Reiquaruim during Ritsuko's breakdown. RITSUKO (OFF): The things here are dummies. And nothing more than parts for Rei. Humanity found a god and, in their joy, tried to make her theirs. That's why there was divine retribution. That was fifteen years ago. The God they had gone to such lengths to find, also vanished completely. But then, they tried to revive the God on their own. That god was Adam. And, from Adam, humans were created in her image. Those were the Evas. Shinji (OFF): People? They're human? RITSUKO: Yes, they're human. The Evas do not intrinsically have souls, but human souls reside within them. They were all salvaged. The only vessel that contained a soul was Rei. She was the only one born with a soul. The Chamber of Guf had been emptied, you see. The Rei-like things here have no souls. They're just vessels. That's why I'm going to destroy them. Because I hate them.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:15 pm Mad-woman talk perhaps, but does she really say anything incorrect? And how many other people would know what their little conclave know about all this ...stuff Yes, they're human. The Evas do not intrinsically have souls, but human souls reside within them. They were all salvaged. The only vessel that contained a soul was Rei. She was the only one born with a soul. The Chamber of Guf had been emptied, you see. The Rei-like things here have no souls. They're just vessels. That's why I'm going to destroy them. Because I hate them.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:20 pm InvaderRen wrote: Honestly, why would you reject that?

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:33 pm If the Chamber of Guf is empty the all Angels throughout the galaxy are fucked. It makes much more sense for each Seed to have their very own Guf.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:37 pm InvaderRen wrote: If the Chamber of Guf is empty the all Angels throughout the galaxy are fucked. It makes much more sense for each Seed to have their very own Guf.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Thu Jan 04, 2007 4:48 pm i was referring to other parts of the series. Im pretty sure we can agree that the Chamber talked about in 2I is different than the one talked about in 3I, no? as for the poem, the subjects are: RITSUKO (OFF): The things here are dummies. Rei And nothing more than parts for Rei. Rei Humanity found a god and, in their joy, tried to make her theirs. Mankind That's why there was divine retribution. Adam/2I That was fifteen years ago. 2I The God they had gone to such lengths to find, also vanished completely. adam/2I But then, they tried to revive the God on their own. Adam/2I That god was Adam. Adam/2I And, from Adam, humans were created in her image. Evangelion Those were the Evas. Evangelion Shinji (OFF): People? They're human? Evangelion RITSUKO: Yes, they're human. Evangelion The Evas do not intrinsically have souls, Evangelion but human souls reside within them. Human souls They were all salvaged. Human souls The only vessel that contained a soul was Rei. Rei She was the only one born with a soul. Rei The Chamber of Guf had been emptied, you see. Lillith's Chamber The Rei-like things here have no souls. Rei They're just vessels. Rei That's why I'm going to destroy them. Ritsuko/Rei Because I hate them. Ritsuko/Rei I outlined the subject of each line. Challenge it if you wish

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Jan 04, 2007 6:17 pm InvaderRen wrote: Mad-woman talk perhaps, but does she really say anything incorrect? And how many other people would know what their little conclave know about all this ...stuff Yes, they're human. The Evas do not intrinsically have souls, but human souls reside within them. They were all salvaged. The only vessel that contained a soul was Rei. She was the only one born with a soul. The Chamber of Guf had been emptied, you see. The Rei-like things here have no souls. They're just vessels. That's why I'm going to destroy them. Because I hate them.
